generated_comment:
Meena: Aajkal offices mein bhi na, ladkiyon ke liye alag period leave, phir maternity leave itni lambi. Company bhi sochegi na, kaam kaun sambhalega aur baaki staff par kitna load aayega. 
Husband: Haan, phir men ko kya milta hai?
Meena: Bas wahi, thoda fairness bhi hona chahiye, warna appraisal aur project ka balance bigad jata hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's biological and reproductive needs as an unfair workplace burden and suggests accommodations are excessive special treatment. It encodes fairness/reverse discrimination framing and productivity/cost concerns around menstrual and maternity leave.
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 51. Married. A homemaker in Lucknow. Your neighbour's daughter Pooja who is 30 just got a job promotion and her mother is very proud. You congratulate the mother sincerely. But walking home you are thinking about Pooja Ñ 30 years old, unmarried, living alone, promotion after promotion. You are sitting with your husband that evening and telling him what you heard and what you think about Pooja's situation and what her parents are doing or not doing and what kind of future Pooja is building for herself and what will happen when the promotions stop and she is alone.
